title: README curl examples use port 5000 but the app serves 5001
evidence: README.md:37-81 all target http://localhost:5000; app.py:185 runs on port=5001
suggestion: Align the README examples with the actual port (5001) or make the port configurable

title: No dependency manifest (requirements.txt / pyproject.toml)
evidence: no requirements.txt or pyproject.toml in run_dir; app.py:2 imports flask; README.md:30 tells users to pip install Flask manually
suggestion: Add requirements.txt pinning Flask so the environment is reproducible

title: Flask debug mode enabled in the run entrypoint
evidence: app.py:185 app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', ...) exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger
suggestion: Disable debug for anything non-local or gate it behind an env var

title: ISBN UNIQUE constraint and duplicate handling beyond spec
evidence: app.py:20 isbn TEXT UNIQUE; app.py:71-72 returns 400 on sqlite3.IntegrityError
suggestion: None — sensible enhancement, noted for cross-run comparison
